storyInfo: Deceived by White's words to 'eliminate the great evil before dealing with the culprit', Chun encourages Jie to die on Wang's behalf. Jie entrusts her writing knife to Jun before she departs.

attributes.content: Damn, it's just a sword. How could this be screwed up? Getting rusty?
attributes.name: Nian

attributes.content: The smith scratches her head in confusion and takes out another steel ingot from storage.
Autumn is wet this year. It's suffocating.
attributes.name: Nian

attributes.content: Something doesn't feel right...
attributes.name: Nian

attributes.content: Impossible... this is impossible!
attributes.name: Dusk

attributes.content: Is It... awakening?
attributes.name: Dusk

attributes.content: (Worried screech)—?
attributes.name: Ink Spirit

attributes.content: An angry cloud has appeared on the calm scroll.
A crack of thunder leaves an ink trail on the white scroll, looking decidedly out of place.
Countless terrible ink spirits appear out of nowhere, scattering across the scroll, covering everything on it in black ink.
I didn't paint this...
attributes.name: Dusk

attributes.content: Go... away!
attributes.name: Dusk

attributes.content: You've had a long journey, Mr. Shuo.
attributes.name: Baizao Soldier

attributes.content: I will take you to a quiet place where you can rest.
attributes.name: Baizao Soldier

attributes.content: I should not delay in reporting to the Ministry of War.
attributes.name: Shuo

attributes.content: There has been an incident in Baizao. His Majesty commands that you are not to enter for the time being.
attributes.name: Baizao Soldier

attributes.content: Why?
attributes.name: Shuo

attributes.content: Is that... the Tomb of Sui?
attributes.name: Shuo

attributes.content: Wang... what have you done?
attributes.name: Shuo

attributes.content: Did the heart skip a beat, or did it beat twice in an instant?
Or was it the vibration of the nomadic city from some sudden movement?
Or was it...
*Plop*
The scroll that was in the proxy's hand is now on the ground.
Wang?!
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: J-Jie!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: What is it?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Look at this!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: A letter of indictment... for Wang? Who gave you this?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Someone sent it to the Sui Regulator. The Supervisor had to report to His Majesty. There are rumors that the Imperial Guard is coming to the Sky Mirror Pavilion!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.text: Finally...
...Finally!
You're here.

attributes.content: Why are you... awake?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: No... no. Your aura is almost identical to Its own, but you've appeared in that hollow visage...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.text: The hollow visage that is also yours.

attributes.content: In any case, you're not awake. You're still in the dream.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.text: Of course I'm awake... you had awakened me centuries ago, when you intruded upon my dream.

attributes.content: You're bluffing... I would not be standing here, if you were truly awake.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: You flexed in your dream and thought you had woken up.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: You, a mere dreamwalker not yet awake, dare call yourself awake?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.text: Dreamwalker? Very well, we'll call this a journey in a dream.
But I see those games you and your siblings play with those puny humans, and your foolish, flawed, presumptuous plan to replace me.
You want to become the same as the humans, to become such fickle, puny creatures, in order to escape your fate?
How pathetic... how laughable!

attributes.content: You forget that these puny creatures defeated you and imprisoned you for a thousand years.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Does it matter if you're awake? You're a shadow, a reflection—a phantom questioning its own shade, a reflection in fleeting water...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Killing you will not be difficult.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.text: Is that so?

attributes.content: The proxy turns around to look at the corridor that he just walked through, but finds no path.

attributes.text: Why are you afraid, then?
It took so long for you to get a chance to see me like this. If you are not afraid, why have you not torn me to pieces in your hatred?
Pathetic... foolish.

attributes.content: Watch your tongue.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.text: Hahahahahahaha!
Didn't you want to fight me to the death?
Then come. Do what you do best.

attributes.content: A weiqi board appears in front of Wang, with a familiar crack on it.
Without asking, the mysterious foe takes the white stone and goes first according to tradition.
The first stone is placed in the Tianyuan position—the center of the board.
First move, Tianyuan.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Are you mocking me?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.text: You'll find out when the game has ended.
Your turn.

attributes.content: He takes a black piece. The nineteen-by-nineteen board is intimately familiar to him; he has not tasted defeat in a long, long time.
Yet it seems as though his opponent sees through his every move, every thought, every emotion.
He soon finds himself at a disadvantage, even though his opponent had forfeited the opening move.
Impossible...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.text: Why would it be?
Have you not realized that all the intricate moves that you have pondered, all your puny stratagems, are no secrets to me?

attributes.content: Because I am you.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: Giving up already? No, no, that won't do.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: You must finish the game.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: So be it.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: He clenches his teeth and picks up another stone.
Every move causes a splitting headache. Every move drains his spirit.
The game is finally concluded after another hundred moves or so, with all endgame plays settled. Black wins by the slimmest of margins—half a point—as the points are tallied.
The player is exhausted, at his last gasp.
(Pants feebly)
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: You lose.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Arrogant as ever...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Haha... hahahahaha!
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: Leaving aside whether this child's play even counts... do you think this is the only game between us?
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: The name of this game is Wang. You may have won this round, but have you any idea how insignificant it is in the big picture?
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: Step back. Go there and look back, and see if there is any meaning in these moves that took every last drop of your being.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: Against his will, Wang gets up and takes a few steps back.
It is only a few steps, but the board that he has been seeing has shrunk to a tiny piece.
No matter how you flail and struggle, it is no more than one-twelfth of the game.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: All the twists and turns of your black pieces have not determined the outcome of a single corner. Why do you think you can make me concede?
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: Wang forces himself to look...

It is no longer a nineteen-by-nineteen board, but hundreds of lines vertical and across. The game that he had poured his heart and soul into just to win by a hair looks utterly insignificant on the vast board.

Countless white pieces, as many as there are stars in the sky, lie beyond the board, which itself seems endless. There is no room to intervene in their formation—as immutable as Sui's anger and contempt, which have simmered for a thousand years.
You planned this...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Of course I planned this.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: This plan began to take form in my countless dreams, ever since you glimpsed into my mind centuries ago.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: A White would begin to lay the pieces around you, quietly and unbeknownst to you.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: What you have done, he made a note of. What you did not finish, he finished for you. What you did not do, he did in your image.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: He made you wary of the humans, and the humans wary of you, forcing you and your kind to return here... this source of your immortality for centuries.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: Why do you care that we live?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: As long as I live, you will always have a choice, the last thing that Yan wants to see—to return to my consciousness, and destroy everything that was built upon the Great Hunt.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: As long as you live, I will never regain my full strength, strength that will allow me to break my shackles.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: Our lives are too intertwined for peace to be a possibility... yet you would seek peace with the humans?
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: Yan would not abide me, who almost became their god. Why would they abide you when you replace me?
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: You are not worthy to play at this board... I will take your life, then the lives of your 'siblings'.
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: Vanish, now, as your inevitable fate—
attributes.name: Wang?

attributes.content: The True Lung let Wang go to the Tomb of Sui alone...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: He would not have given Wang the opportunity to tell me...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: He never wanted to hurt anyone.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Words were never his strong suit. He was obsessed with victory and defeat, but cared nothing for loss and gain... the shrewdest, and the most foolish amongst us.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: He saw through every scheme and treachery, but he could not do something as simple as letting those around him truly know him.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Besides... am I not the proxy that the Sui Regulator, and you, have always feared the most?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I knew. Or rather, I knew they would tell you.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I can feel it, even if you didn't say anything...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I thought I could control this power in time. Lately, though, I'm feeling less and less like myself...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: My memories began to grow indistinct many years ago. In recent years, the same has happened to my emotions.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Perhaps...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: There's something I want to ask you, Chun, impolite as it may be.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: What did you feel when your mother died?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Underneath her thick black hair, the Elf's ears twitched.
Why do you ask?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Because there is still hope for Wang.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: You're going to save him?!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: As it stands, with my strength and his, I can do no more than die in his place.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: What is the death of a family member like, for beings like you and me?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Why do you ask? Why do you have to open my wounds—
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Suddenly, Chun understands.
Jie is asking for herself, and for Wang.

If losing a loved one is merely a little speck of sorrow in a long life, then she will choose death, and leave the insignificant sorrow to Wang, and become a loss that stings a little from time to time for her siblings.

If the pain of losing a loved one still threatens to tear one apart, even for those with lifespans far beyond the ordinary mortal, then she will choose to bear the unbearable pain, and the burden of those left behind, seeing the task of eliminating Sui through to the end.
An immortal being, facing the possibility of losing family forever for the first time.
Though she has written about meetings and partings innumerable times, finding no other measure to judge by, she can only rely on Chun to make a decision.
She only has to tell the truth.
That the pain of losing a loved one does not heal so easily, even after decades, after centuries.
That death is liberation and life is pain. Which way the scale tips is obvious.
That it was best for Wang to let him go, that Jie should remain and feel the pain that lingers. Wang reaps the death that he sowed while Jie remains. It is how things should be.
That Jie would cautiously advance the cause of eliminating Sui, without crossing the line as Wang did, incurring the wrath of the True Lung when the Sui Regulator received an anonymous tip.
But—
Eliminate the great evil, before dealing with the culprit.'
There is no one in the Sky Mirror Pavilion or the Imperial Historical Cabinet who can stop her. It's becoming a real problem.'
She should be held responsible if the history of Yan falls into chaos!'
Imperial Historical Cabinet abolished. Takes control of the written language of Yan. No one may oppose her.'
Reviews the livelihood of every household in the land. Anyone who has committed fraudulent acts has their name erased and is exiled to the borders. Thousands die.'
Chaotic, murky memories grip the Candleholder's heart.
It is not right. She seems to remember that none of the speakers had faces.
Something is not right. It is false, fabricated, designed to drive a rift between Candleholder and proxy, turn them against each other.
But... is it not a fact that Jie's power is growing?
She will become more powerful, more terrible, than the Sui beast itself. She cannot help it. I must help her. I must do my duty to Great Yan—
Two entwined wills surge in her chest. One belongs to her, the other to those faceless people, but also...
...To a Yan that does not want to give a proxy complete control over history.
She cannot help but suspect that her recruitment by the Sui Regulator was not luck, but planned.
She was assigned to watch the most difficult proxy to control, because of her long lifespan.
She would understand the monster that Jie would become, because of her interest in history.
She would lose her hard-earned home if she betrayed Great Yan, because she has nowhere to go.
Is that why?
She feels the crushing weight of her burden. A rumbling sound fills her mind, and she cannot think.
She must do her duty to Yan, do her duty as Candleholder. By chance, she has seen Jie at her most fragile. She must let the great evil take the culprit's place.
It would be best for the proxies, even. Yan would lose a reason to wage war on them—
My mother...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: When she passed... I felt...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: I felt... very little.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: <color=#d41f1f>The pain tore you apart.</color>
I felt as though the death of my mother left a hole in my heart, made me find something new to fill it.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: <color=#d41f1f>It was as though your heart was ripped out from your chest, leaving behind a bloody hole, and you know that nothing would ever fill it, that things would never be the same again.</color>
I cried when I buried her, but... that was it. I stopped crying very soon.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: <color=#d41f1f>You had cried your tears dry by the time the last dirt was laid over your mother's face, but still you could not stop crying. You had no idea what to do.</color>
Our... our journey is a long one. We'll find new things and meet new people; that will soothe the temporary pain.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: <color=#d41f1f>Your life is too long, and the lasting things too few. When you lose them, they leave behind voids that can never be filled, wounds that never heal.</color>
I hardly even think of my mother anymore, because I have... you... I have... other friends.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: <color=#d41f1f>Your loved ones appear in your dreams. They come to you when you are alone.</color>
<color=#d41f1f>The pain grips your heart when you hear songs like the melodies that she once hummed. There is nowhere to run.</color>
When I'm with you... I'm...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: ...I'm happy.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: She barely managed to utter those last few words, so great was her urge to throw up. She could hardly breathe.
She dares not look into the proxy's eyes.
She fears that the one before her would be blinded by her words and truly believe that loss is something that can be soothed by time.
She fears that the one before her would see through her lies, see her vile intention and despise her.
...She fears herself, for saying those words.
I see...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I see. Thank you.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: You're a good Candleholder.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: The murk in the Candleholder's chest suddenly clears. There is only an empty hole there now.
Wind blows through the hole and speaks in her voice.
Jie, I...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: It was a cruel question. But I asked because my heart was in turmoil.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: On second thought, I would likely have made the same decision, whether I asked the question or not. I regretted it as soon as I asked.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: You're smart. You would have realized that I was forcing my Candleholder to choose my death or Wang's.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I shouldn't have made you do this.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: You didn't say anything about yourself. You chose to communicate Great Yan's will to me, that's all.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Death is an adventure that is unique for everyone. There is no previous experience to draw from. We must each walk our own path.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: The local annals that needed to be edited are on my desk. Take them to the Sui Regulator, when you have time.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: ...Jie!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Chun fights back her tears. Even crying would be a lie, here and now.
But the hole in her chest continues to sob, a sound that only she can hear. Finally, she forces a few sorrowful, powerless words from her chest.
I... I'll be going.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: You...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: ...Take care.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: What's going on, Jie? The Imperial Guard is coming to the Sky Mirror Pavilion, and I almost got shut inside my own home by my Candleholder—
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: ...Did something happen to Wang?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: You can hear it, can't you?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Its... heartbeat.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: He went into the Tomb of Sui?!
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: When?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Two hours ago.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: We can talk about the rule-breaking later. The court clearly wants to exclude us, and time is short. What's your plan?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I'm going to help Wang.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I'll go with you.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: No.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Why?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: He and I will take care of this.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Are you sure?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I'm sure.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: He and I have been making plans to eliminate Sui. There is no time to explain the plan right now.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Your presence will not help; it will only increase the risk.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Besides, there's something that I must ask you to do.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Take care of this writing knife.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Your powers should be able to preserve it.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: What are you planning? How bad have things gotten?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: It will not awaken, not yet. I can promise you this. And so can Wang.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: But you...
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: ...Someone will come back.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Either you or him?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I'm coming with you.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: No, Jun.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I truly mean no offense. I need to entrust this writing knife to the keeping of someone who is absolutely just and impartial.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: If someone with ill intent gets their hands on it... it will do far more damage than the death of a single proxy.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I thought about destroying it. In the end, I felt that it was worth preserving.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: The wrongs of the past should be righted, though I do not know at what price... perhaps the writing knife will be of help to you, or to Yan.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Until the day comes when no one can use it anymore.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Don't be like this, Jun.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Maybe I'll come back with Wang.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Then we'll go together to eat at Yu's, and listen to you sing...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: And if I never return...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: There's a long road ahead of you...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: It is not so easy to not look back.
She looks at the innumerable lives and deaths recorded upon the shelves, many of which she had written with her own pen.
Would she become one of them?
How would those who come after depict this life, one that seems too long for others to comprehend, yet far too hurried for herself?
How would the other siblings react when they read those pages?
No. There will be no record of her. She knows it.
Of all the siblings, she is the one who has spent the most time with Wang.
She has given Wang ideas for his plans, helped him calculate the conditions and timing for Sui's elimination.
She can guess what is happening in the Tomb of Sui. The clear and distinct heartbeat has already told the full story.
There is no other way.
She must use her powers to erase the name—
She has spent much more time in the mortal realm than ordinary people.
Everyone dies. It is precisely the cycle of life and death that made possible the innumerable ways of life that have fascinated her, as well as her siblings.
But if death is such an unremarkable thing, like a wisp of smoke in the wind... what is living, truly?
Light from the setting sun pours into the room, upon the countless history tomes, as though relentlessly poring through traces of the past.
Every word in these scrolls was written with tears of blood, but the only thing written on the last page is the word 'regret'.

attributes.text: The sun's rays by the window...
\n\n...How warm.

attributes.content: But she does not walk towards the sunlight.
She leaves the room.
Into the darkness of the tomb.
No words need to be said. Wang knows it is a fragment It has retrieved from his own memory, an image that he has never forgotten for even a moment in the last hundred and twenty years.
He lies exhausted on the ground. A hundred white pieces on the board have turned into blades that impale his chest, and he is slowly inundated by the pure white.
Until the golden light surrounds him.
...I'm sorry.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Wait for me... wait for me to come back.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: For you to come back.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: It was the other side of the dream, a place that he should not have been able to touch.
But she seems to notice him and turns around to look at him.
There is a faint smile on Jie's face, and she nods lightly.
The light gushes from behind her, surrounding the figure, swallowing it, dissolving it...
Sweeping away the dream realm, and revealing the true face of this place.
